verses.dev: स प्राङ्मुखो राजगृहादभिनिर्याय राघवः।ततस्सुदामां द्युतिमान् सन्तीर्यावेक्ष्य तां नदीम्ह्लादिनीं दूरपारां च प्रत्यक्स्रोतस्तरङ्गिणीम्।शतद्रूमतरच्छ्रीमान्नदीमिक्ष्वाकुनन्दनः
verses.trans: Glorious and prosperous prince Bharata, the delight of the Ikshvaku race, set out from Rajagriha, took the eastern direction, and observing the course of the river Sudama crossed Hladini and Satadru rivers which were very wide and flowing westwards crested with waves.

words.m: the delight of the Ikshvaku race

verses.dev: स प्राङ्मुखो राजगृहादभिनिर्याय राघवः।ततस्सुदामां द्युतिमान् सन्तीर्यावेक्ष्य तां नदीम्ह्लादिनीं दूरपारां च प्रत्यक्स्रोतस्तरङ्गिणीम्।शतद्रूमतरच्छ्रीमान्नदीमिक्ष्वाकुनन्दनः
verses.trans: Glorious and prosperous prince Bharata, the delight of the Ikshvaku race, set out from Rajagriha, took the eastern direction, and observing the course of the river Sudama crossed Hladini and Satadru rivers which were very wide and flowing westwards crested with waves.

words.m: the delight of the Ikshvaku race

verses.dev: ऐलाधाने नदीं तीर्त्वा प्राप्य चापरपर्पटान्।शिलामकुर्वतीं तीर्त्वा आग्नेयं शल्यकर्षणम्सत्यसन्धश्शुचिश्श्रीमान्प्रेक्षमाण श्शिलावहाम्।अत्ययात्स महाशैलान्वनं चैत्ररथं प्रति
verses.trans: Bharata, ever true to his word, handsome and purehearted, reached Aparaparpata region and crossed river Satadru at Eladhana, the mountain in which river Silavaha originates. Then he traversed Salyakarsna regions in the northeast, observing the course of the Silavaha and went beyond the great mountains in the direction of the forest called Chaitraratha.

words.m: one who is always true to his word

verses.dev: ऐलाधाने नदीं तीर्त्वा प्राप्य चापरपर्पटान्।शिलामकुर्वतीं तीर्त्वा आग्नेयं शल्यकर्षणम्सत्यसन्धश्शुचिश्श्रीमान्प्रेक्षमाण श्शिलावहाम्।अत्ययात्स महाशैलान्वनं चैत्ररथं प्रति
verses.trans: Bharata, ever true to his word, handsome and purehearted, reached Aparaparpata region and crossed river Satadru at Eladhana, the mountain in which river Silavaha originates. Then he traversed Salyakarsna regions in the northeast, observing the course of the Silavaha and went beyond the great mountains in the direction of the forest called Chaitraratha.

words.m: one who is always true to his word

verses.dev: सरस्वतीं च गङ्गां च युग्मेन प्रतिपद्यच।उत्तरान्वीरमत्स्यानां भारुण्डं प्राविशद्वनम्
verses.trans: Arriving at the confluence of Saraswati and Ganga and traversing the north of Veeramatsya region, he entered the Bharunda forest.

verses.dev: वेगिनीं च कुलिङ्गाख्यां ह्लादिनीं पर्वताऽऽवृताम्।यमुनां प्राप्य सन्तीर्णः बलमाश्वासयत्तदा
verses.trans: He crossed the swiftflowing river known as Kulinga surrounded by hills and pleasing to the mind. Thereafter on crossing Yamuna he made his army rest on the otherside.

verses.dev: सशीतीकृत्य तु गात्राणि क्लान्तानाश्वास्य वाजिनः।तत्र स्नात्वा च पीत्वा च प्रायादादाय चोदकम्
verses.trans: The weary horses rested and cooled their bodies. And they bathed and drank. They set out again refreshed taking with them a store of water.

verses.dev: राजपुत्रो महारण्यमनभीक्ष्णोपसेवितम्।भद्रो भद्रेण यानेन मारुतः खमिवात्ययात्
verses.trans: Then with the blessed prince (Bharata) on an excellent chariot, it (the army) moved through the vast and uninhabited forest like the god of wind passing through the sky.

verses.dev: यन्मे धनं च रत्नं च ददौ राजा परन्तपः।परिश्रान्तं पथ्यभवत्ततोऽहं पूर्वमागतः
verses.trans: The (horses carrying) the wealth including the gems which the king (of Kekaya), the subduer of enemies had bestowed on me were exhausted on the way. Therefore I have come in advance.

verses.dev: स गङ्गां प्राग्वटे तीर्त्वा समायात्कुटिकोष्ठिकाम्।सबल स्तां स तीर्त्वाऽथ समायाद्धर्मवर्धनम्
verses.trans: With Ganga at Pragvata city and river Kutikoshtika crossed along with his army, he reached Dharmavardhana village.

verses.dev: तोरणं दक्षिणार्धेन जम्भूप्रस्थमुपागमत्।वरूथं च ययौ रम्यं ग्रामं दशरथात्मजः
verses.trans: Having traversed the southern end of Torana, the son of Dasaratha (Bharata) reached the beautiful Varutha village via Jambuprastha.

verses.dev: तत्र रम्ये वने वासं कृत्वाऽसौ प्राङ्मुखो ययौ।उद्यानमुज्जिहानायाः प्रियका यत्र पादपाः
verses.trans: He (Bharata) halted in that charming forest for a while, proceeded eastwards till he reached a place known as Ujjihana garden abounding in spriyaka trees.

verses.dev: सालांस्तु प्रियकान्प्राप्य शीघ्रानास्थाय वाजिनः।अनुज्ञाप्याथ भरतः वाहिनीं त्वरितो ययौ
verses.trans: After reaching the groves of sala and priyaka trees, Bharata harnessed the swiftrunning horses to the chariot and ordered his forces to proceed fast.

verses.dev: वासं कृत्वा सर्वतीर्थे तीर्त्वा चोत्तानिकां नदीम्।अन्या नदीश्च विविधाः पार्वतीयैस्तुरङ्गमैःहस्तिपृष्ठकमासाद्य कुटिकामत्यवर्तत।ततार च नरव्याघ्रो लौहित्ये स कपीवतीम्
verses.trans: That tiger among men, Bharata halted at a place known as Sarvatirtha and crossed the Uttanika and various other rivers with the help of hillborn horses. He crossed the Kutika river on an elephant the river Kapivati at a place known as Lauhitya.

words.m: in a place known as Sarvatirtha

verses.dev: वासं कृत्वा सर्वतीर्थे तीर्त्वा चोत्तानिकां नदीम्।अन्या नदीश्च विविधाः पार्वतीयैस्तुरङ्गमैःहस्तिपृष्ठकमासाद्य कुटिकामत्यवर्तत।ततार च नरव्याघ्रो लौहित्ये स कपीवतीम्
verses.trans: That tiger among men, Bharata halted at a place known as Sarvatirtha and crossed the Uttanika and various other rivers with the help of hillborn horses. He crossed the Kutika river on an elephant the river Kapivati at a place known as Lauhitya.

words.m: in a place known as Sarvatirtha

verses.dev: एकसाले स्थाणुमतीं विनते गोमतीं नदीम्।कलिङ्गनगरे चापि प्राप्य सालवनं तदाभरतः क्षिप्रमागच्छत्सुपरिश्रान्तवाहनः।
verses.trans: Bharata crossed the rivers Sthanumati at Ekasala and Gomati at Vinata villages. Thereafter as his horses were extremely tired he rested for a while in the sala forest in Kalinganagara before he speedily set out (again).

verses.dev: वनं च समतीत्याशुशर्वर्यामरुणोदयेअयोध्यां मनुना राज्ञा निर्मितां सन्ददर्श ह।
verses.trans: He passed quickly through the forest at night and at sunrise beheld the city of Ayodhya built by king Manu.

verses.dev: तां पुरीं पुरुषव्याघ्रस्सप्तरात्रोषितः पथिअयोध्यामग्रतो दृष्ट्वा सारथिं वाक्यमब्रवीत्।
verses.trans: Having spent seven nights on his way, that tiger among men (Bharata) saw Ayodhaya before him. He said to the charioteer:

verses.dev: एषा नातिप्रतीता मे पुण्योद्याना यशस्विनीअयोध्या दृश्यते दूरात्सारथे पाण्डुमृत्तिका।यज्वभिर्गुणसम्पन्नैर्ब्राह्मणैर्वेदपारगैःभूयिष्ठमृद्धैराकीर्णा राजर्षिपरिपालिता।
verses.trans: O charioteer I am able to see Ayodhya of great renown from a distance but not so clearly that whiteclaycity of Ayodhya, with its sacred gardens ruled by several rajarsis, inhabited by numerous wealthy people, by virtuous brahmins and priests who perform sacrifices and who are wellversed in the Vedas.

words.m: by those well versed in the vedas

verses.dev: एषा नातिप्रतीता मे पुण्योद्याना यशस्विनीअयोध्या दृश्यते दूरात्सारथे पाण्डुमृत्तिका।यज्वभिर्गुणसम्पन्नैर्ब्राह्मणैर्वेदपारगैःभूयिष्ठमृद्धैराकीर्णा राजर्षिपरिपालिता।
verses.trans: O charioteer I am able to see Ayodhya of great renown from a distance but not so clearly that whiteclaycity of Ayodhya, with its sacred gardens ruled by several rajarsis, inhabited by numerous wealthy people, by virtuous brahmins and priests who perform sacrifices and who are wellversed in the Vedas.

words.m: by those well versed in the vedas

verses.dev: अयोध्यायां पुरा शब्दश्श्रूयते तुमुलो महान्समन्तान्नरनारीणां तमद्य न श्रुणोम्यहम्।
verses.trans: I do not hear now, in Ayodhya the tumultous noise of men and women I used to hear earlier all over.

verses.dev: उद्यानानि हि सायाह्ने क्रीडित्वोपरतैर्नरैःसमन्तात्परिधावद्भिः प्रकाशन्ते ममान्यथा।
verses.trans: I do not see people who with passion used to stroll in the parks early in the morning. I see no cheerfulness in any one.

verses.dev: तान्यद्यानुरुदन्तीव परित्यक्तानि कामिभिःअरण्यभूतेव पुरी सारथे प्रतिभाति मे।
verses.trans: O charioteer, people full of passion have abandoned the gardens. To me, the city looks like a forest as if weeping.

verses.dev: न ह्यत्र यानैर्दृश्यन्ते न गजैर्न च वाजिभिःनिर्यान्तो वाऽभियान्तो वा नरमुख्या यथापुरम्।
verses.trans: Important people mounted on elephants or horses are not seen going out of the city or coming in.

verses.dev: दानयज्ञाधिकारा हि शीलश्रुतिवचोऽनुगा।बुद्धिस्ते बुद्धिसम्पन्न प्रभेवार्कस्य मन्दिरे
verses.trans: O highly intelligent one, by your wisdom, virtuous conduct and scriptural knowledge you are entitled to dispense charity and perform sacrifices. You will always shine incessantly like light of the Sun.

words.m: ever following virtuous conduct and scriptures
entitled to  dispense charity and perform sacrifices

verses.dev: तान्येतान्यद्य पश्यामि निरानन्दानि सर्वशःस्रस्तपर्णैरनुपथं विक्रोशद्भिरिव द्रुमैः।
verses.trans: Now the trees appear cheerless, the paths are full of dry leaves fallen all over. It appears as if the trees are crying.

verses.dev: नाद्यापि श्रूयते शब्दः मत्तानां मृगपक्षिणाम्संरक्तां मधुरां वाणीं कलं व्याहरतां बहु।
verses.trans: I do not hear in the red tincture of the rising Sun the melodious voices of the freely moving animals and birds.

words.m: in the red tincture (of the rising Sun)

verses.dev: चन्दनागरुसम्पृक्तो धूपसम्मूर्छितोऽतुलःप्रवाति पवन श्श्रीमान्किन्नुनाद्य यथापुरम्।
verses.trans: Why is it that the incomparable fragrance of sandal and incense that used to fill the nostrils, not blowing now in the prosperous city of Ayodhya?

verses.dev: भेरीमृदङ्गवीणानां कोणसङ्घट्टितः पुनःकिमद्य शब्दो विरतस्सदा दीनगतिः पुरा।
verses.trans: Why is it that the sounds of musical instruments like mrudangas and veenas are not heard now? Why is the city filled with melancholy?

words.m: of the trumpets, mrudangas and veena

verses.dev: न नूनं मां महाराजः प्राप्तं जानाति कीर्तिमान्।उपजिघ्रेद्धि मूर्ध्नि तात स्सन्नम्य सत्वरम्।।2,72.30।।
verses.trans: Surely the illustrious maharaja is not aware of my arrival. Otherwise he would have quickly bent my head and kissed me on my forehead.

verses.dev: सर्वथा कुशलं सूत दुर्लभं मम बन्दुषुतथाह्यसति सम्मोहे हृदयं सीदतीव मे।
verses.trans: I do not see anywhere, O charioteer the wellbeing, of my kinsmen. Hence I feel deluded and my heart is filled with grief.

verses.dev: यो मे भ्राता पिता बन्धुर्यस्य दासोऽस्मि धीमतः।तस्य मां शीघ्रमाख्याहि रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः
verses.trans: Announce at once my arrival to the wise Rama, the man of unwearied actions, my brother, father and friend whose slave I am.

words.m: I am a slave (of that wise man)
of a man of unwearied actions

verses.dev: द्वारेण वैजयन्तेन प्राविशच्छ्रान्तवाहनःद्वास्स्थैरुत्थाय विजयं पृष्टस्तै स्सहितो ययौ।
verses.trans: He entered the city through the gate known as Vaijayanta with his horses exhausted. The doorkeepers stood up and amidst cries of victory followed him in.

verses.dev: धर्मविद्धर्मनित्यश्च सत्यसन्धो दृढव्रतः।आर्यः किमब्रवीद्राजा पिता मे सत्यविक्रमः
verses.trans: What did my father, the noble king, conversant with righteous conduct, true, to his vows, one who always conducted himself in a righteous manner, whose truth was his prowess (at the time of his death)?

words.m: one who always coducts himself in a righteous manner
did he say (at the time of his death)?

verses.dev: किमहं त्वरयाऽनीतः कारणेन विनानघअशुभाशङ्कि हृदयं शीलं च पततीव मे।
verses.trans: O irreproachable one, why was I brought here in haste without any reason? My mind apprehends something inauspicious. I feel indisposed.

verses.dev: श्रुता नो यादृशाः पूर्वं नृपतीनां विनाशनेआकारांस्तानहं सर्वानिहपश्यामि सारथे।
verses.trans: O charioteer I now see the very for ebodings I had heard in the past in connection with the death of kings.

verses.dev: सम्मार्जनविहीनानि परुषाण्युपलक्षयेअसंयत कवाटानि श्रीविहीनानि सर्वशः।बलिकर्मविहीनानि धूपसम्मोदनेन चअनाशितकुटुम्बानि प्रभाहीनजनानि च।अलक्ष्मीकानि पश्यामि कुटुम्बिभवनान्यहम्
verses.trans: I see the unswept homes of householders standing dirty with doors not closed. There is no beauty anywhere. No one offers oblations (at the time of worship). There is no fragrance of burning incense. The families have no food to eat. The people look cheerless. I see inauspiciousness everywhere.

verses.dev: सम्मार्जनविहीनानि परुषाण्युपलक्षयेअसंयत कवाटानि श्रीविहीनानि सर्वशः।बलिकर्मविहीनानि धूपसम्मोदनेन चअनाशितकुटुम्बानि प्रभाहीनजनानि च।अलक्ष्मीकानि पश्यामि कुटुम्बिभवनान्यहम्
verses.trans: I see the unswept homes of householders standing dirty with doors not closed. There is no beauty anywhere. No one offers oblations (at the time of worship). There is no fragrance of burning incense. The families have no food to eat. The people look cheerless. I see inauspiciousness everywhere.

verses.dev: तच्छ्रुत्वा विषसादैव द्वितीयाप्रियशंसनात्।विषण्णवदनो भूत्वा भूयः पप्रच्छ मातरम्
verses.trans: Bharata was more distraught on hearing the words that conveyed the second unpleasant tidings. With downcast countenance, he again enquired of his mother thus:

words.m: the account of second unpleasant tidings

verses.dev: अपेतमाल्यशोभानि ह्यसम्मृष्टाजिराणि च।देवागाराणि शून्यानि न चाभान्ति यथापुरम्
verses.trans: The temples stand devoid of the beauty of floral offerings. The floors of front yards are left unswept and unsmeared. They look deserted, devoid of brightness of the past.

words.m: deprived of the splendour of garlands
floors of the front yards unsmeared

verses.dev: तथा पृष्टा यथातत्त्वमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे।मातास्य सुमहद्वाक्यं विप्रियं प्रियशङ्कया
verses.trans: Thus questioned, his mother started relating the exact events, presuming them to be pleasant tidings (for Bharata).

verses.dev: दृश्यन्ते वणिजोऽप्यद्य न यथापूर्वमत्रवै।ध्यानसंविग्नहृदयाः नष्टव्यापारयन्त्रिताः
verses.trans: Even traders seem restrained by the loss of business. They are not seen as before concentrating on their trade.

words.m: restrained by the loss of business

verses.dev: देवायतनचैत्येषु दीनाः पक्षिगणास्तथामलिनं चाश्रुपूर्णाक्षं दीनं ध्यानपरं कृशम्।सस्त्रीपुंसं च पश्यामि जनमुत्कण्ठितं पुरे
verses.trans: I see groups of birds frequenting the temples. The sanctuaries appear dispirited. The people in the city, both men and women look dull, emaciated, pitiable with throats choked. Their eyes are filled with tears and minds absorbed in distressing thoughts.

verses.dev: देवायतनचैत्येषु दीनाः पक्षिगणास्तथामलिनं चाश्रुपूर्णाक्षं दीनं ध्यानपरं कृशम्।सस्त्रीपुंसं च पश्यामि जनमुत्कण्ठितं पुरे
verses.trans: I see groups of birds frequenting the temples. The sanctuaries appear dispirited. The people in the city, both men and women look dull, emaciated, pitiable with throats choked. Their eyes are filled with tears and minds absorbed in distressing thoughts.

verses.dev: कच्चिन्न परदारान्वा राजपुत्रोऽभिमन्यते।कस्मात्स दण्डकारण्ये भ्रूणहेव विवासितः
verses.trans: I hope he did not covet another man's wife. Like a sinner who had slain a child in embryo why has he been banished to Dandaka forest?

words.m: like a sinner who had slain a child in embryo

verses.dev: अथास्य चपला माता तत्स्वकर्म यथातथम्।तेनैव स्त्रीस्वभावेन व्याहर्तुमुपचक्रमे
verses.trans: Then the capricious mother, with the same feminine disposition which she had displayed earlier, started giving a faithful account of all her deeds.

verses.dev: बहूनि पश्यन्मनसोऽप्रियाणि यान्यन्यदा नास्य पुरे बभूवुः।अवाक्छिरा दीनमना नहृष्टः पितुर्महात्मा प्रविवेश वेश्म
verses.trans: Having witnessed many unpleasant things that had never happened before, the magnanimous Bharata who was looking cheerless and dejected entered his father's palace with his head bent down. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे एकसप्ततितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the seventyfirst sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

verses.dev: न ब्राह्मणधनं किञ्चिद्धृतं रामेण कस्यचित्कश्चिन्नाढ्यो दरिद्रो तेनापापो विहिंसितः।न रामः परदारांश्च चक्षुर्भ्यामपि पश्यति
verses.trans: Rama did not rob any brahmin of even a little wealth nor did he harm any innocent man, whether rich or poor. He never casts his eyes on another's wife.

verses.dev: मया तु पुत्र श्रुत्वैव रामस्यैवाभिषेचनम्।याचितस्ते पिता राज्यं रामस्य च विवासनम्
verses.trans: My son, I on hearing of Rama's consecration asked your father to confer the kingdom on you and banish Rama.

verses.dev: स स्ववृत्तिं समास्थाय पिता ते तत्तऽथाकरोत्।रामश्च सह सौमित्रिः प्रेषितस्सह सीतया
verses.trans: Your father, faithful to his word, banished Rama along with Lakshmana and Sita.

verses.dev: तमपश्यन्प्रियंपुत्रं महीपालो महायशाः।पुत्रशोकपरिद्यूनः पञ्चत्वमुपपेदिवान्
verses.trans: The renowned ruler of the earth (king Dasaratha), unable to behold (the sorry state of) his beloved son and stricken by filial grief, merged into the five elements (died).

words.m: the state of merger in the five elements

verses.dev: त्वयात्विदानीं धर्मज्ञ राजत्वमवलम्ब्यताम्।त्वत्कृते हि मया सर्वमिदमेवं विधं कृतम्
verses.trans: O knower of dharma, assume kingship now. It is only for your sake that all this has been done by me.

verses.dev: मा शोकं मा च सन्तापं धैर्यमाश्रय पुत्रक।त्वदधीना हि नगरी राज्यं चैतदनामयम्
verses.trans: My son, give up sorrow and agony and take recourse to courage. This city as well as the kingdom, free from all obstacles, is under your control.
